title: erythroxylum ellipticum
intro: Erythroxylum ellipticum is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae, native to South America, and is also known as the "Willow-leaf Coca" due to its similarity in appearance to the coca plant, but it is not a member of the coca family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Erythroxylum ellipticum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as potentially alleviating symptoms of anxiety and depression. Erythroxylum ellipticum is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with some herbalists also using the leaves in poultices or salves to treat skin conditions.
